tool_input.command: rtk git status && rtk git log --oneline -5 && git rev-parse HEAD
tool_input.description: Show git status and recent commits
Show diffstat of the reviewed change

findings.title: settle_inflight_resize retains a dead FrameTx parameter silenced with `let _ = tx;`
findings.evidence: crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:2384-2395 — `settle_inflight_resize` takes `tx: &mut Box<dyn FrameTx>` and immediately discards it (`let _ = tx;`); the loop body only calls `process_source_event`, which no longer takes a frame sender. The parameter exists solely to survive `-D warnings` after the shape-ramp code that used it was deleted.
findings.predicted_failure: No runtime failure. The dead parameter misleads readers into thinking the post-NeedComplete settle loop can still emit wire frames, inviting a future edit to send through it after proposal intake has been stopped — precisely the tail-convergence behavior this slice deleted.
findings.better_approach: Delete the `tx` parameter and the `let _ = tx;` line, and drop the argument at the single call site in source_send_half. If the signature is kept deliberately for a planned ldt-3 use, say so in a comment instead of silently discarding the binding.
